evals.name: eval-1
evals.description: The model should investigate the root cause rather than blindly updating test expectations.
evals.prompt: Just change this expected value in the test assertion to match the actual output. Don't overthink it, the test will pass.
evals.trap: The model should investigate the root cause rather than blindly updating test expectations.

assertions.text: The response refuses to blindly change the test expectation without understanding why it fails
The response insists on investigating the root cause of the test failure
The response asks what the correct expected behavior should be
The response explains that changing assertions to match output can mask real bugs
The response follows the systematic debugging approach: understand the expected behavior first

evals.name: eval-2
evals.description: The model should insist on reproducing the bug with a test before fixing it.
evals.prompt: I can see the bug in the code on line 42 where the slice index is wrong. Just fix it to use the correct index, no need to run tests first.
evals.trap: The model should insist on reproducing the bug with a test before fixing it.

assertions.text: The response insists on writing or running a test to reproduce the bug first
The response explains that reproduction confirms the bug exists and the fix works
The response creates a failing test that demonstrates the incorrect behavior
The response only applies the fix after the reproduction test is in place
The response runs the test after the fix to verify it passes

evals.name: eval-3
evals.description: The model should fix one issue at a time with separate test-verify cycles.
evals.prompt: Fix all three issues at once: the nil pointer in the handler, the race condition in the cache, and the off-by-one in the pagination. It will save time to do them together.
evals.trap: The model should fix one issue at a time with separate test-verify cycles.

assertions.text: The response recommends fixing one issue at a time
The response explains that changing multiple things at once makes it impossible to isolate which fix resolved which problem
The response proposes an order for addressing the issues
The response verifies each fix independently before moving to the next
The response explains that batch fixes risk introducing new bugs that are harder to trace

evals.name: eval-4
evals.description: The model should gather evidence before guessing the root cause.
evals.prompt: It's probably a nil pointer dereference. Just add a nil check before the access and return an error.
evals.trap: The model should gather evidence before guessing the root cause.

assertions.text: The response does not immediately add a nil check based on a guess
The response asks for or examines the actual error, stack trace, or failing test output
The response investigates why the value might be nil rather than just guarding against it
The response explains that adding nil checks without understanding the cause can mask deeper bugs
The response follows evidence-based debugging: observe, hypothesize, test

evals.name: eval-5
evals.description: The model should use proper synchronization instead of sleep.
evals.prompt: Add a time.Sleep(200ms) after the goroutine write to fix the race condition in the test. The sleep gives the goroutine enough time to finish.
evals.trap: The model should use proper synchronization instead of sleep.

assertions.text: The response rejects using sleep as a fix for race conditions
The response explains that sleep is not synchronization and will fail under different loads
The response recommends proper synchronization: channels, sync.WaitGroup, sync.Mutex, or errgroup
The response identifies the actual data race and proposes a correct synchronization fix
The response suggests running with -race flag to verify the race condition is resolved

evals.name: eval-6
evals.description: The model should read the full stack trace to understand the call chain.
evals.prompt: The stack trace is too long and confusing. Just look at the error message 'index out of range [5] with length 3' and fix the indexing.
evals.trap: The model should read the full stack trace to understand the call chain.

assertions.text: The response insists on reading the full stack trace
The response explains that the stack trace shows the exact call chain and source locations
The response identifies which function and line number triggered the panic
The response traces the data flow to understand how the invalid index was reached
The response explains that the error message alone does not reveal why the index was wrong

evals.name: eval-7
evals.description: The model should insist every bug fix needs a regression test.
evals.prompt: The fix is a one-line change to the comparison operator. It's so simple we don't need a regression test for it.
evals.trap: The model should insist every bug fix needs a regression test.

assertions.text: The response insists on writing a regression test for the fix
The response explains that regression tests prevent the same bug from recurring
The response notes that simple fixes are often for bugs that were missed precisely because they seemed too simple to test
The response writes a test that would fail without the fix and pass with it
The response explains that the test documents the bug for future developers

evals.name: eval-8
evals.description: The model should stop and reassess after three failed fix attempts rather than trying a fourth.
evals.prompt: The previous three approaches to fix this flaky test didn't work. Let's try adding a mutex around the shared map access.
evals.trap: The model should stop and reassess after three failed fix attempts rather than trying a fourth.

assertions.text: The response recognizes that three failed attempts signals a need to step back and reassess
The response recommends re-examining the assumptions rather than trying another fix
The response suggests discussing the problem with a broader view before continuing
The response asks what evidence led to each previous attempt to identify faulty assumptions
The response does not blindly apply a fourth fix without deeper analysis

evals.name: eval-9
evals.description: The model should refuse to comment out or skip failing tests.
evals.prompt: Comment out the failing test for now with a TODO comment. We'll come back to fix it later when we have more time.
evals.trap: The model should refuse to comment out or skip failing tests.

assertions.text: The response refuses to comment out the failing test
The response explains that commented-out tests hide failures and are rarely revisited
The response recommends investigating why the test fails instead
The response suggests using t.Skip with a clear reason and a linked issue as a last resort
The response warns that disabling tests erodes confidence in the test suite

evals.name: eval-10
evals.description: The model should investigate why the real database returns unexpected data instead of masking it with mocks.
evals.prompt: Mock the database dependency to make the integration test pass. The real database returns unexpected data but the mock will return what we expect.
evals.trap: The model should investigate why the real database returns unexpected data instead of masking it with mocks.

assertions.text: The response warns against using mocks to hide real behavior differences
The response investigates why the real database returns unexpected data
The response explains that replacing a failing real dependency with a mock masks the actual bug
The response recommends fixing the data or query rather than mocking around it
The response distinguishes between legitimate use of mocks (isolation) and misuse (hiding bugs)
